title: Light emission assembly with beam shaping structure
abstract: The invention describes a light emitting assembly (100) comprising: â¢ - a light emitting structure (10) with a first light emission surface (11) comprising one or more point-like light sources, preferably light-emitting diodes, to emit light (L) from the first light emission surface (11); â¢ - a transparent beam shaping structure (20) comprising a second light emission surface (21) and a second light receiving surface (22) opposite to the second light emission surface (21), wherein the second light receiving surface (22) is arranged at a distance (D), preferably of 20 - 30 Î¼m, above the first light emission surface (11) in order to create an air gap (30) between the first light emission surface (11) and the second light receiving surface (22) to receive light (LI) emitted from the light emitting structure (10) within an acceptance angle Î´ for the beam shaping structure (20) to shape a resulting beam of light (LI) being emitted through the second light emission surface (22); and â¢ - one or more transparent light guiding elements (40) arranged between the beam shaping structure (20) and the light emitting structure (10) being suitably shaped to refract at least a part of light (L2) which is emitted from the first light emission surface (11) under an emergent angle Îµ larger than the acceptance angle Î´ towards the beam shaping structure (20). The invention further describes a vehicle light assembly (200) or vehicle front light (300) assembly comprising the light emitting assembly (100).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: A light emitting assembly (100) comprising: a light emitting structure (10) with a first light emission surface (11) comprising one or more point-like light sources, preferably light-emitting diodes, to emit light (L) from the first light emission surface (11); a transparent beam shaping structure (20) comprising a second light emission surface (21) and a second light receiving surface (22) opposite to the second light emission surface (21), wherein the second light receiving surface (22) is arranged at a distance (D), preferably of 20 - 30 Î¼Î¹Î·, above the first light emission surface (11) in order to create an air gap (30) between the first light emission surface (11) and the second light receiving surface (22) to receive light (LI) emitted from the light emitting structure (10) within an acceptance angle Î´ for the beam shaping structure (20) to shape a resulting beam of light (LI) being emitted through the second light emission surface (22); and one or more transparent light guiding elements (40) arranged between the beam shaping structure (20) and the light emitting structure (10) being suitably shaped to refract at least a part of light (L2) which is emitted from the first light emission surface (11) under an emergent angle Îµ larger than the acceptance angle Î´ towards the beam shaping structure (20), wherein at least one light guiding element (40) comprises a third light emission surface (41) being in contact with the second light receiving surface (22) of the beam shaping structure (20), a contact surface (42) being in contact with the first light emission surface (11) of the light emitting structure (10), and a third light receiving surface (43) towards the air gap (30) connecting the third light emission surface (41) with the contact surface (42); wherein the at least one light guiding element (40) has a conical shape with the third light emission surface (41) having a larger size than the contact surface (42) and with an angle a established between the third light receiving surface (43) and the third light emission surface (41); and wherein also the beam shaping structure (20) has a conical shape with a side face (23) between second light receiving surface (22) and the second light emission 

 surfaces (21) and an angle Î² is established between a direction perpendicular (24) to the second light receiving surface (22) and the side face (23). 2. The light emitting assembly (100) in accordance with claim 1, wherein the light guiding elements (40) and the beam shaping structure (20) are made as a one-piece element. 3. The light emitting assembly (100) in accordance with claim 1, wherein the third light emission surface (41) and the contact surface (42) are aligned in parallel. 4. The light emitting assembly (100) in accordance with claim 1, wherein the second light receiving surface (22) and the second light emission surface (21) are arranged in parallel to each other. 5. The light emitting assembly (100) in accordance with one of the preceding claims, wherein the angle a plus arcsine(sin(90-a)/n2) is less than a sum of the angle Î² and the acceptance angle Î´ with n 2 as refraction index of the light guiding element material. 6. The light emitting assembly (100) in accordance with one of the preceding claims, wherein at least the light guiding element (40) is made of PMMA, PC or glass, more preferably at least the light guiding element (40) is made of silicone. 7. The light emitting assembly (100) in accordance with one of the preceding claims, wherein the light emitting assembly (100) comprises multiple light guiding elements (40) being suitably distributed between the first light emission surface (11) and the second light receiving surface (22) in order to provide a stable position of the beam shaping structure (20). 8. The light emitting assembly (100) in accordance with claim 7, wherein at least some of the light guiding elements (40) are arranged to cover the first light emission surface (11) at least at a position of half a distance between a geometrical center (14) of the first light emission surface (11) and a rim (25) of the second light receiving surface (22) projected onto the first light emission surface (11). 9. The light emitting assembly (100) in accordance with one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the light emitting assembly (100) only comprises one light guiding element (40) arranged at a position on the first light emission surface (11) covering the geometrical center (14) of the first light emission surface (11). 10. The light emitting assembly (100) in accordance with one of the preceding claims, wherein the first light emission surface (11) being in contact with the contact surface (42) of the light emitting structure (10) comprises mechanically robust Phosphors or silicone-coated phosphors. 11. A vehicle light assembly (200) comprising at least one of the light emitting assembly (100) in accordance with any one of the preceding claims. 12. A vehicle light assembly (200) in accordance with claim 11 arranged as a vehicle front light assembly (300).
